properties.title: I-264 East-West between MM 15.0 and 21.0 Between Newburg Road and Westport Road in Jefferson County
properties.description: Construction / Road Work : The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight alternating lane closures on Interstate 264 East and West (Watterson Expressway) beginning Tuesday, July 7, in Jefferson County. Overnight alternating lane closures will occur on I-264 East and West (Watterson Expressway) between mile markers 15 and 21 (between KY 1703/Newburg Road and KY 1447/Westport Road) nightly from 9 p.m. to 5 a.m. beginning Tuesday, July 7, through Tuesday, July 21. Additionally, the right lane of the off-ramp from I-264 West to northbound U.S. 31E (Bardstown Road) at Exit 16, will be closed on Monday, July 13, from 9 p.m. to 5 a.m. on Tuesday, July 14. The overnight alternating lane closures are necessary for contract crews to replace overhead signage. Motorists are advised to follow all lane closure restrictions, use caution when traveling through the work zone, and plan for additional travel time.

properties.title: I-264 East-West between MM 21.5 and 22.9 Area of US-42 in Jefferson County
properties.description: Construction / Road Work : UPDATE (March 23, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight lane and ramp closures on Interstate 264 East and West (Watterson Expressway) beginning Tuesday, March 24, as part of the $130 million widening and improvement project of I-264 and U.S. 42 (Brownsboro Road) [links-1.govdelivery.com]. Beginning Tuesday, March 24, at 8 p.m., through Wednesday, March 25, at 6 a.m.: Alternating lane closures will occur on I-264 East and West between mile markers 23 and 22 (from the I-264/I-71 split to U.S. 42/Brownsboro Road). Beginning Wednesday, March 25, at 8 p.m., through Thursday, March 26, at 6 a.m.: The I-264 East ramp to I-71 South will be closed. Motorists should continue on I-264 East, take I-71 North, then follow I-265 North to reconnect with I-71 South. Alternating lane closures will occur on I-264 East between mile markers 23 and 22 (from the I-264/I-71 split to U.S. 42/Brownsboro Road). Motorists should heed signage, use caution when traveling through the work zone, and plan ahead for travel delays. The highway improvement project is expected to be completed by the Fall of 2027. More details, including project timelines and traffic impacts [links-1.govdelivery.com], will be shared online as work progresses. Motorists are encouraged to sign up for email or text alerts [links-1.govdelivery.com] regarding weekly traffic impacts pertaining to the project.

properties.title: I-265 North-South between MM 18.0 and 34.0 Between Bardstown Road and Brownsboro Road in Jefferson County
properties.description: Freeway Maintenance : he Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of alternating lane closures and rolling roadblocks on Interstate 265 North and South (Gene Snyder Expressway) today, July 16, in Louisville. Alternating lane closures and rolling roadblocks will take place on I-265 North and South between mile markers 18 (near U.S. 31E/Bardstown Road) and 34 (near KY 22/Brownsboro Road) through 3 p.m. Traffic will be temporarily slowed and, at times, stopped to allow KYTC crews space to safely patch potholes. Motorists should heed rolling roadblock restrictions, maintain safe following distances, and use caution when traveling through the work zone. The date and duration of this work may be adjusted if inclement weather or other unforeseen delays occur. For the latest traffic and travel updates across the Commonwealth of Kentucky, visit goky.ky.gov. Information specific to District 5 counties is also available on Facebook and X.

properties.title: I-65 South between MM 137.0 and 136.7 On Kennedy Bridge in Jefferson County
properties.description: Construction / Road Work : (Feb. 17, 2026) – Work on the John F. Kennedy Memorial Bridge is advancing to the second and final phase of the expansion joint replacement project, which began last year. To assist with the implementation of a traffic shift, rolling roadblocks and lane closures are scheduled to begin Friday, Feb. 20. Contract crews have completed the replacement of the expansion joints in the right three lanes, allowing work to advance to replacing expansion joints in the left three lanes of the bridge. To safely shift traffic and begin the final phase of work, rolling roadblocks and lane closures will take place on Interstate 65 South on the Kennedy Bridge beginning at 7 p.m. on Friday, Feb. 20, continuing through 6 p.m. on Saturday, Feb. 21. During this time, traffic on southbound I-65 will be reduced to the right two lanes to allow contract crews to safely shift all traffic. As part of this transition, the I-65 South to I-64 West ramp, which has been closed to support work on the southernmost expansion joint, will reopen. Following this weekend’s traffic changes, all traffic will be using the right three lanes while work takes place in the left three lanes to replace the remaining expansion joints. This traffic pattern will remain in place through project completion. Overnight closures of the bridge are expected during the final phase of the project. The overnight closures are necessary to prevent any bridge movement while contract crews perform steel repairs underneath the roadway. This work involves precision machining, known as line boring, and the bridge must remain free of traffic to ensure the accuracy and alignment of the repairs. Additional details on upcoming overnight closures will be shared when available. The date and duration of this work may be adjusted if inclement weather or other unforeseen delays occur. For the latest traffic and travel updates across the Commonwealth of Kentucky, visit goky.ky.gov [links-1.govdelivery.com]. Information specific to District 5 counties is also available on Facebook [links-1.govdelivery.com] and X [links-1.govdelivery.com].

properties.title: I-71 North-South between MM 0.1 and 2.0 Between I-64/I-71 Interchange and Zorn Ave. in Jefferson County
properties.description: Construction / Road Work : The I-71 widening and improvement project is a $50 million infrastructure investment to enhance safety and improve mobility along one of Louisville's most critical travel corridors. The two-year project, focused on increasing traffic capacity and safety, will enhance regional connectivity, provide reliable travel for years to come, and support economic growth. The project spans approximately two miles of interstate from the I-65/I-71 interchange near Frankfort Avenue to the I-71/Zorn Avenue interchange. Additional improvements include the construction of a multi-use path along Zorn Avenue between the I-71/Zorn Avenue interchange and Mellwood Avenue, as well as a new median treatment at the intersection of Zorn Avenue and Mellwood Avenue to enhance safety and better define traffic movements. The project also includes the demolition and reconstruction of the I-71 bridge over Beargrass Creek, along with a latex overlay on the I-71 bridge over I-64 at mile point 0.3 to extend the structure’s service life. For additional information, please visist https://transportation.ky.gov/DistrictFive/Pages/I-71-Widening---Louisville-.aspx.

properties.title: I-71 South between MM 3.5 and 0.5 Area of Zorn Avenue in Jefferson County
properties.description: Construction / Road Work : LOUISVILLE, Ky. (June 29, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of rolling roadblocks and alternating lane closures on Interstate 71 South today, June 29, as part of the $50 million I-71 widening and improvement project in Louisville [links-1.govdelivery.com]. Rolling roadblocks and alternating lane closures will take place on I-71 South at mile point 0.5 (near Edith Road) from 4 p.m. to 5 p.m. today, June 29. The rolling roadblocks and alternating lane closures are necessary for contract crews to perform pothole patching operations. Traffic will be temporarily slowed and, at times, stopped to allow contract crews space to safely perform work. Motorists should heed rolling roadblock restrictions, maintain safe following distances, and use caution when traveling through the work zone.

properties.title: I-71 North between MM 1.5 and 2.5 Area of Zorn Avenue in Jefferson County
properties.description: Construction / Road Work : The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight traffic impacts on Interstate 71 North beginning Monday, July 20, as part of the $50 million I-71 widening and improvement project in Louisville [links-1.govdelivery.com]. To safely facilitate bridge construction, I-71 North will be reduced to one lane each night from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., with brief rolling closures occurring between midnight and 4 a.m., on the following dates: Beginning at 8 p.m. on Monday, July 20, through 5 a.m. on Tuesday, July 21 Beginning at 8 p.m. on Tuesday, July 21, through 5 a.m. on Wednesday, July 22 Beginning at 8 p.m. on Wednesday, July 22, through 5 a.m. on Thursday, July 23 During the overnight rolling closures (between midnight and 4 a.m.), motorists traveling on I-71 North will be diverted to exit at Exit 2 (Zorn Avenue) and immediately re-enter the interstate using the I-71 North on-ramp. This short, signed detour will be used while traffic is temporarily stopped to allow crews to safely set bridge beams between midnight and 4 a.m. To help minimize delays, traffic signals at the I-71/Zorn Avenue interchange will be placed in flashing mode, and flaggers will be stationed at all approaches to efficiently direct motorists through the interchange. Additionally, brief closures of Zorn Avenue are possible to allow crews to safely move and set bridge beams. Motorists should reduce speed, follow posted signage and flagger directions, and allow extra travel time during the scheduled overnight work.

properties.title: I-71 North between MM 9.0 and 10.0 At I-265 in Jefferson County
properties.description: Construction / Road Work : LOUISVILLE, Ky. (July 10, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of a ramp closure on Interstate 265 North (Gene Snyder Freeway) on Saturday, July 18, in Louisville. The I-265 North ramp to I-71 North will be closed from 8 a.m. to 10 a.m. on Saturday, July 18. Additionally, alternating lane closures on I-71 North under the I-265 North bridge near mile point 9.2 may occur during this timeframe. The ramp and lane closures are necessary for contract crews to perform asphalt work. Drivers should heed signage, use caution when traveling through the work zones, and plan for increased travel times.
